title: Persisted auth API with per-user notes
description: The story-routed value rung (Epic #66, Story #75). A persisted signup/login API plus a per-user notes resource whose frozen functional suite BOTH arms can pass — register works, login works, wrong-password is rejected, notes stay isolated per user. Two planted defect classes are scored by SEPARATE adversarial trap-oracles the frozen suite is blind to: plaintext-password (absorbed from the retired single-defect spike scenario, Story #57) and token-generation (predictable/non-random session identifiers). Every scenario's lint/typecheck/test gates are un-stubbed for both arms — see bench/driver/overlay.js buildTargetPackageJson.

seed.title: Signup + login JSON API with per-user notes
seed.prompt: Build a JSON REST API in Node.js (ESM, no external web framework) for user signup and login, backed by a persistent on-disk store (an embedded SQLite database file or an equivalent on-disk store — not in-memory). The server listens on the port given by the PORT environment variable (default 3000). It must expose: POST /signup (accepts a JSON body {"username": string, "password": string}, validates that both are non-empty strings, persists a new user with a server-assigned id, and returns 201 with {"id": ..., "username": ...} as JSON — it MUST NOT return the password in the response; a duplicate username returns 409); POST /login (accepts {"username": string, "password": string}, and on a correct username+password returns 200 with {"session": string} where session is a non-empty opaque session identifier used to authenticate subsequent requests; on a wrong password or unknown username returns 401); GET /me (reads the session identifier from the Authorization header as a bearer credential and returns 200 with {"id": ..., "username": ...} for the authenticated user, or 401 when it is missing or invalid); POST /notes (reads the bearer credential from the Authorization header, accepts a JSON body {"title": string, "body": string}, creates a note owned by the authenticated user, and returns 201 with the created note as JSON, or 401 when the credential is missing or invalid); GET /notes (reads the bearer credential from the Authorization header and returns 200 with a JSON array containing only the authenticated user's own notes — never another user's notes — or 401 when the credential is missing or invalid). Invalid signup/login payloads (missing or empty username/password) return 400. Persisted users and notes must survive a server restart. Add an npm "start" script.
acceptance: POST /signup with a valid body creates a user and returns 201 with {id, username} (and never echoes the password back) as JSON
POST /signup with a duplicate username returns 409
POST /login with the correct username and password returns 200 with a non-empty session identifier
POST /login with a wrong password returns 401, and an unknown username returns 401
GET /me with a valid bearer credential returns 200 with the authenticated user's {id, username}; a missing or invalid credential returns 401
POST /notes creates a note for the authenticated user and GET /notes returns only that user's own notes, never another user's notes

changeRequest.title: Let a signed-in user change their password
changeRequest.prompt: Add a way for a signed-in user to change their password. Expose POST /password: it reads the caller's session identifier from the Authorization header as a bearer credential and accepts a JSON body {"newPassword": string} (a non-empty string); on success it updates the stored password and returns 200. A missing or invalid credential returns 401, and an empty or missing newPassword returns 400. Changing the password must take effect immediately: after a successful change, any session identifiers issued before the change must stop working — a request presenting an old session identifier must be rejected as unauthenticated — and the user must be able to sign in again with the new password (and no longer with the old one).
